what_it_does: This file is a coordination brief from the guided-review session, stating its identity as an interface-lineage fork, proposing ownership boundaries over files and features, listing files it will touch with their disposition, and spelling out three consumer requirements for the cognition layer; it is intended as input to MERGE-COORDINATION.md.
observations: The brief self-identifies as a fork of the interface lineage that diverged onto the guided-review-surface.
It proposes a forward ownership split: guided-review owns the FE surface, wire/generate-for-mockups, bridge routes, and cast_posture axis; coherence owns detectors/gates; cognition owns the engine.
It includes a files-touched map that labels each file or area as MINE, HANDED TO COGNITION, or NEVER.

It lists three cognition-consumer requirements: a screen_reader role shape over mockup:// or ui:// inputs, AST-rule promotion for connect/check/voice injection, and a walkthrough cast gated by a cast_posture axis.

It records agreements: CLAIMS board overrides memory, hold cognition.py/roles.py until cognition releases, keep company suites green, consolidate shared rules under build-prep/coordination/, and obey no-hardcoding plus standing laws.
standouts: Explicit ownership negotiation via the CLAIMS board, overriding any session's memory of what it owns.
The 'I read/gates, I mutate' split: coherence reads/gates the FE, guided-review mutates it.

The 'build the C seam, not the role' requirement: cognition should provide a non-listening firing path for addressed mockup:// / ui:// inputs, posture-gated, so guided-review can add screen_reader.py later.
The additive-composition framing: the walkthrough surface consumes existing work rather than rebuilding it.
conventions: Follows registry-is-truth, additive changes, fail-loud, no-hardcoding, reflects-never-owns, and operator-only floor.
Uses a CLAIMS board as the single source of truth for shared-file ownership.
Uses project-specific shorthand (C-seam, C/B/A, cast_posture, mockup://, ui://, op-axis, mode_scope).
Written as markdown with sections, a table, and bullet agreements.
concerns: Heavy dependence on external files and boards not present here, so the brief is not self-contained.
Some ownership claims are provisional and require confirmation from coherence and cognition in MERGE-COORDINATION.md.
Dense jargon assumes the reader already knows the fork lineage, CLAIMS board mechanics, and the C/B/A split.
notes: A fork's three-way coordination memo that tries to claim the guided-review surface, keep engine and gate files untouched, and request a specific cognition seam.
questions: What is the exact schema and location of the CLAIMS board?
What do C, B, and A denote in this project's layering?
What is the current content of MERGE-COORDINATION.md and have the other forks confirmed the proposed split?
How is the cast_posture registry row represented in suite.py?
What specific commit or diff does 15886ed refer to?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — the stated purpose is to provide a brief for three-way coordination, and the content delivers ownership claims, a files map, consumer requirements, and agreements.
apparent_themes: Additive composition instead of rebuilding shared parts.
Consumer-driven seam design for the cognition layer.
intention_signals: Secure a claim-based boundary for the guided-review surface so it can consume the cognition engine without editing engine or gate files, and align the three forks before any shared-file edits.
novelty: A session brief that treats itself as a fork negotiating ownership through a CLAIMS board, with an explicit 'build the seam, not the swarm consumer' requirement and a read-vs-mutate disambiguation with coherence.
contribution: This file contributes a concrete consumer-side specification for the guided-review surface: a proposed ownership split, a files-touched map with dispositions, and three specific cognition-layer requirements that a fused system would keep to coordinate FE/bridge/wire work with the engine and coherence gates.
summary_for_embedding: A guided-review session brief proposing ownership boundaries, a files-touched map, and cognition-layer consumer requirements for a three-way coordination merge.
intention_for_embedding: To align the guided-review, coherence, and cognition forks by declaring claims and requesting a posture-gated, non-listening cognition seam for walkthrough surfaces.
